body:
Follow-up from the #292 mixed-era placement audit (item 2 of 5).

## Goal

Provide a single placement-expansion helper so the "walk every fragment to its holding D-server" pattern has one definition and the raw-`placement` iteration that caused the rebalance bug (see the rebalance evacuation issue) cannot recur.

## Change

Add to `ChunkRef` (`crates/core/src/metadata.rs`, alongside `fragment_count()` and `placed_dserver()`):

```rust
/// Every fragment of this chunk paired with its holding D server, applying the
/// identity-placement fallback for pre-M3 / mixed-era records. The single
/// "walk every fragment to its D-server" expansion.
pub fn fragments(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = (u16, DServerId)> + '_ {
    (0..self.fragment_count()).map(move |i| (i, self.placed_dserver(i)))
}
```

Then route the consumers that hand-roll this expansion through it:

- GC `referenced_fragments` (`crates/custodian/src/gc.rs:179`) — currently `for index in 0..chunk.fragment_count() { ... chunk.placed_dserver(index) ... }`.
- Reconstruction `assess` (`crates/custodian/src/reconstruction.rs:226`) — currently builds the same vector by hand.
- Rebalance `plan_evacuations` once it is fixed (sibling issue).

Update the `placed_dserver` doc comment (`metadata.rs:110-118`) to list **all** callers, including rebalance.

## Test

Unit-test `fragments()` for `EcScheme::None` and `ReedSolomon{k,m}` across empty, full, and (per the strictness decision) short placement vectors, asserting it matches the read path's `placed_dserver` resolution for every index.

## Notes

This is the "one placement-expansion helper" named in the #292 expected output. `placed_dserver` / `fragment_count` remain its primitives; the read path keeps using `placed_dserver` directly (it resolves one index at a time).


---

## Addition (post-#292 exhaustiveness sweep): enforce "no raw placement iteration" in CI

ADR-0040 (#355) states the raw-iteration defect class "structurally cannot recur … a reviewer can grep for `.placement.iter()`." Make that real rather than manual: add a CI guard — a `cargo xtask` grep gate, or a clippy `disallowed_methods` / lint — that **fails the build if `ChunkRef.placement` is iterated or indexed directly** outside the `fragments()` / `placed_dserver` helpers (and the write path that constructs it). This is the standing prevention that keeps a future consumer from re-introducing the rebalance (#346) or fan-out (#356) divergence after this helper lands.

milestone.title: M3 — Custodians
milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: Add ChunkRef::fragments() placement-expansion helper and route all consumers through it
